title: arabis hispida var. hastula
intro: Arabis hispida var. hastula, also known as the rock cress or frost cress, is a perennial plant species within the genus Arabis. It is a member of the Brassicaceae family, commonly referred to as the mustard family, and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain and swelling. Arabis hispida var. hastula can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be beneficial for respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems, and can be consumed raw in salads or as a garnish.
